title: Kaizen Savings Tracker
description: Industrial-grade tool for calculating cost savings from Kaizen (continuous improvement) events, aligned with Lean, Six Sigma, and ISO 9001 standards. Tracks direct savings, productivity gains, quality improvements, and hidden loss drivers.

inputs.label: Labor Rate (per hour)
inputs.businessContext: Average fully loaded labor cost per operator hour, including wages, benefits, and overhead. Based on industry benchmarks (BLS, ISO 9001:2015 clause 7.1.6).

businessContext_i18n.en: Average fully loaded labor cost per operator hour, including wages, benefits, and overhead. Based on industry benchmarks (BLS, ISO 9001:2015 clause 7.1.6).

inputs.label: Number of Operators Affected
inputs.businessContext: Number of direct operators whose work is impacted by the Kaizen event. Used to calculate labor savings.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of direct operators whose work is impacted by the Kaizen event. Used to calculate labor savings.

inputs.label: Time Saved per Operator per Shift
inputs.businessContext: Reduction in cycle time or non-value-added time per operator per shift, measured via time studies (Lean standard work, ISO 22400).

label_i18n.en: Time Saved per Operator per Shift

businessContext_i18n.en: Reduction in cycle time or non-value-added time per operator per shift, measured via time studies (Lean standard work, ISO 22400).

inputs.label: Shifts per Day
inputs.businessContext: Number of production shifts per day. Typical in manufacturing (WERC DC Measures).

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of production shifts per day. Typical in manufacturing (WERC DC Measures).

inputs.label: Operating Days per Year
inputs.businessContext: Annual operating days, excluding weekends and holidays. Standard for industrial planning (ISO 9001).

businessContext_i18n.en: Annual operating days, excluding weekends and holidays. Standard for industrial planning (ISO 9001).

inputs.label: Defect Rate Before Kaizen
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of defective units before improvement. Six Sigma DMAIC baseline (DPMO conversion).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of defective units before improvement. Six Sigma DMAIC baseline (DPMO conversion).

inputs.label: Defect Rate After Kaizen
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of defective units after improvement. Must be <= defect_rate_before for valid savings.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of defective units after improvement. Must be <= defect_rate_before for valid savings.

inputs.label: Annual Production Volume
inputs.businessContext: Total units produced per year. Used to scale defect savings.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total units produced per year. Used to scale defect savings.

inputs.label: Cost per Defect
inputs.businessContext: Average cost of rework, scrap, warranty, and inspection per defective unit (COPQ, ISO 9001:2015 clause 9.1.3).

businessContext_i18n.en: Average cost of rework, scrap, warranty, and inspection per defective unit (COPQ, ISO 9001:2015 clause 9.1.3).

inputs.label: Material Cost Savings (annual)
inputs.businessContext: Direct material savings from waste reduction, scrap reduction, or better yield. Enter 0 if not applicable.

businessContext_i18n.en: Direct material savings from waste reduction, scrap reduction, or better yield. Enter 0 if not applicable.

inputs.label: Energy Cost Savings (annual)
inputs.businessContext: Energy savings from process improvements (ISO 50001, Lean energy). Enter 0 if not applicable.

businessContext_i18n.en: Energy savings from process improvements (ISO 50001, Lean energy). Enter 0 if not applicable.

inputs.label: Implementation Cost (one-time)
inputs.businessContext: Total cost to implement the Kaizen (training, equipment, downtime). Used for ROI calculation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total cost to implement the Kaizen (training, equipment, downtime). Used for ROI calculation.

inputs.label: Sustainability Factor
inputs.businessContext: Confidence that savings will persist over time. Low = 0.7, Medium = 0.85, High = 1.0. Based on Lean sustainability audits.

businessContext_i18n.en: Confidence that savings will persist over time. Low = 0.7, Medium = 0.85, High = 1.0. Based on Lean sustainability audits.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Quality of input data. Low = 0.8, Medium = 0.9, High = 1.0. Affects adjusted savings.

businessContext_i18n.en: Quality of input data. Low = 0.8, Medium = 0.9, High = 1.0. Affects adjusted savings.

rules.message: Defect rate after Kaizen must be less than or equal to defect rate before.
Time saved per operator must be non-negative.
Annual production volume must be greater than zero.

thresholds.message: Total annual savings are below $10,000. Consider re-evaluating the Kaizen scope.
ROI is negative. The implementation cost exceeds first-year savings.
More than 50% of savings come from defect reduction. Verify defect rate data accuracy.
